title: Be Verb (am/is/are/was/were/will be)
description: Master the most important verb in English — 'Be'. Learn am/is/are (present), was/were (past), will be (future), being, been, contractions, questions, negatives, and use with adjectives, nouns, and prepositions.

subtopics.title: Am / Is / Are — Present Tense
subtopics.description: I am, You are, He/She/It is — present state, identity, profession.

subtopics.title: Was / Were — Past Tense
subtopics.description: I was, We were — describing past states and situations.

subtopics.title: Will Be — Future Tense
subtopics.description: I will be, It will be — talking about future states.

subtopics.title: Being — Continuous Forms
subtopics.description: She is being rude. It is being fixed. Progressive use of be.

subtopics.title: Been — Perfect Forms
subtopics.description: I have been, She has been, We had been — perfect aspect.

subtopics.title: Be Verb in Questions
subtopics.description: Are you ready? Was he there? Will it be okay? Forming questions.

subtopics.title: Be Verb in Negatives
subtopics.description: I am not, She isn't, They weren't — negative structures.

subtopics.title: Be + Adjectives
subtopics.description: She is tall. He was angry. Using be to describe qualities.

subtopics.title: Be + Nouns (Identity)
subtopics.description: He is a doctor. They are students. Defining identity and role.

subtopics.title: Be + Prepositions (Location)
subtopics.description: She is at home. They are in the office. Expressing location.

subtopics.title: Be in Passive Voice
subtopics.description: The letter was written. It is being repaired. Be in passive structures.

subtopics.title: Be Verb in Daily Life
subtopics.description: 100+ real sentences using be verb at home, school, market.

subtopics.title: Be Verb in Professional English
subtopics.description: Office, emails, meetings — professional use of be verb.

subtopics.title: Contractions with Be
subtopics.description: I'm, you're, he's, isn't, aren't, wasn't — spoken and informal English.

learningObjectives: Correctly use am/is/are for present tense
Use was/were for past states and situations
Form future statements with will be
Use being in continuous and passive structures
Use been in perfect tenses (has been, had been)
Form questions and negatives with be verb
Combine be verb with adjectives to describe people and things
Use be verb with nouns to express identity
Use be verb with prepositions to express location
Understand be verb in passive voice constructions
